result.stdout:
Generate an SBOM file and send it to a Metaport server.

Usage: metaport --transport=foo [OPTIONS]...

Options:

--transport     The transport method to use to send the generated SBOM to a Metaport server. One of 'http' or 'email'.
--name          The name of the application whose data will be sent to a Metaport server.
--host          The Metaport server domain or IP address. If "transport=email" is passed, then "host" should be a valid email address.
--uuid          The unique identifier for the application, as provided by your Metaport administrator.
--auth          A base64 encoded Basic Auth combination of the form: <username:password>, as provided by your Metaport administrator (HTTP transport only)
--domain        The primary domain-name of the application whose data will be sent to a Metaport server.
--env           The environment of the application whose data will be sent to a Metaport server e.g. "Prod" or "UAT".
--version       The version of the application whose data will be sent to a Metaport server.
--classic       If passed, the output of dependency analysis will be sent to Metaport.

Example: metaport --transport=http --name=MyApp --host=metaport.dev --uuid=ef029e7e-e97a-4298-83ee-20112139856e --auth='cnVzZ0B0aGVydXNzLm8vbTp0b2FzdG56MTIzNC0=' --domain=myapp.com --env=Prod --version=1.0.1 --classic=1

Author: Dcentrica Solutions Ltd https://dcentrica.com <hello@dcentrica.com>
